title: Proclamation No. 490, s. 1939
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 490

RESERVING FOR SCHOOL PURPOSES FIVE PARCELS OF THE PUBLIC DOMAIN SITUATED IN THE POBLACION AND BARRIOS OF GUINZADAN AND OTUCAN, MUNICIPAL DISTRICT OF BAUKO, SUBPROVINCE OF BONTOC, MOUNTAIN PROVINCE, ISLAND OF LUZON.

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce and pursuant to the provisions of section eighty-three of Commonwealth Act Numbered One hundred and forty-one, as amended, I hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for school purposes, under the administration of the Director of Education, subject to private rights, if any there be, the following parcels of the public domain, situated in the Poblacion and barrios of Guinzadan and Otucan, municipal district of Bauko, subprovince of Bontoc, Mountain Province, Island of Luzon, and particularly described in the Bureau of Lands plan Psu-104506, to wit:

Lot No. 1 (Poblacion). —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Psu-104506, S. 73° 36′ E. 319.10 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Bauko, thence S. 17° 15′ W. 39.S3 m. to point 2; S. 9° 42′ E. 43.35 m. to point 3; S. 73° 43′ E. 42.94 m. to point 4; S. 16° 08′ E. 31.65 m. to point 5; S. 26° 42′ W. 32.39 m. to point 6; S. 80° 52′ W. 13.04 m. to point 7; N. 62° 41′ E. 9.58 m. to point 8; N. 35° 38′ E. 31.66 m. to point 9; N. 50° 11′ W. 154.62 m. to point 10; N. 71° 14′ E. 6.99 m. to point 11; N. 71° 13′ E. 70.89 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 3,354 square meters.

Points 1 and 11, P. L. S. concrete monuments; point 6, P. L. S. stone monument; points 8 and 9, old points; and the rest are P. L. S. concrete monuments, 15 by 60 cm.

Bounded on the northeast, by property of Tomas V. Guerson; on the east, by properties of Tomas V. Guerson and Uyad; on the south, by property of Uyad; on the southwest, by property of the municipal government of Bauko (II-1969) ; and on the northwest, by properties of Jose Dominguez and Tomas V. Guerson.

Lot No. 2 (Poblacion). —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Psu-104506, S. 35° 17′ E. 268.53 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Bauko, Mountain Province, thence N. 0° 25′ E. 31.55 m. to point 2; N. 61° 24′ E. 29.74 m. to point 3; S. 7° 50′ E. 62.00 m. to point 4; S. 35° 31′ E. 70.76 m. to point 5; N. 81° 11′ W. 28.82 m. to point 6; N. 18° 25′ W. 49.86 m. to point 7; N. 19° 39′ W. 18.68 m. to point 8; N: 81° 15′ W. 25.70 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 2,177 square meters.

Points 1, 2, 3, 6, 7, and 8, P. L. S. stone monuments; point 4, old stone monument with cross; point 5, old stone monument with cross on top.

Bounded on the northeast by property of the municipal government of Bauko (11-1969); on the south, by public land; on the southwest, by properties of Pedro Cajegan and Asterio Pagteilan; on the west, by property of Asterio Pagteilan; and on the northwest, by provincial road.

Bearings true. Declination 0° 34′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Psu-104506, sheet No. 1.

Surveyed: November 3, 1937 to March 19, 1938.

Approved: September 27, 1938.

Lot No. 3 (Barrio of Guinzadan). —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Psu-104506, S. 2° 33′ W. 2,878.73 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Bauko, Mountain Province, thence S. 67° 17′ E. 59.71 m. to point 2; S. 29° 06′ W. 34.59 m. to point 3; S. 7° 07′ W. 64.12 m. to point 4; N. 49° 48′ W. 93.61 m. to point 5; N. 36° 06′ E. 69.89 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 5,718 square meters.

Points 1, 2, and 4, P. L. S. stone/monuments; point 3, cross on rock; point 5, rock with cross.

Bounded on the northeast by barrio road; oh the southeast, by property of Batnag, Cantadong, Cablay et al. (joint owners) ; and on the southwest and northwest, by creek (no name).

Bearings true. Declination 0° 30’E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Psu-104506, sheet No. 2.

Surveyed: November 3, 1937 to March .19, 1938.

Approved: September 27, 1938.

Lot No. 4 (Otucan Barrio School Site) .—Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Psu-104506, N. 51° 40′ E. 2,091.86 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Bauko, Mountain Province, thence N. 62° 31′ E. 57.87 m. to point 2; S. 81°J:36′ E. .53,51. m. to point 3; S. 76° 29′ W. 96.00 m. to point 4; S. 23° 25′, E. 18.43 m. to point 5; S. 85° 17′ W. 13.6.1 m. to point 6; N. 11° 58′ W. 10.91 m. to point 7; N. 12° 36′ W. 11.19 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 1,424 square meters.

Point 2, P. L. S. stone monument; point 4, old point; and the rest are P. L. S. stones.

Bounded on the north by property of Quitayan; on the east, by property of the Roman Catholic Church (mission site) ; on the southeast, by property of the Roman Catholic Church (mission site) ; on the south, by property of Pedro Bayot; on the west, by property of Bandodayan; and on the northwest, by property of Selud.

Lot No. 5 (Otucan Barrio School Site). —Beginning at a point marked 1 on Bureau of Lands plan Psu-104506, N. 50 c 46′ E. 2,164.40 m., more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 1, municipal district of Bauko, Mountain Province, thence S. 80° 11′ E. 39.30 m. to point 2; N. 22° 12′ E. 29.60 m. to point 3; S. 72° 41′ E. 46.59 m. to point 4; S. 38° 24′ W. 32.60 m. to point 5; S. 41° 41′ W. 29.03 m. to point 6; S. 53° 36′ W. 42.34 m. to point 7; N. 37° 22′ W. 66.21 m. to point 8; N. 76° 30′ E. 3.00 m. to point 9; N. 9° 05′ E. 53.50 m. to point 10; S. 11° 13′ E. 41.43 m. to point 1, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 4,879 square meters.

Points 8 and 9, old points; and the rest are P. L. S. stones.

Bounded on the north by properties of Pedro Basalong and Selud; on the east, by property of Pedro Basalong; on the southeast, by creek (no name) ; on the southwest, by property of Felix Malidong; and on the west, by properties of the Roman Catholic Church (mission site) and Pedro Basalong.

Bearings true. Declination 0° 44′ E.

Points referred to are marked on Bureau of Lands plan Psu-104506, sheet No. 3.

Surveyed: November 3, 1937 to March 19, 1938.

Approved: September 27, 1938.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done at the City of Manila, this fifth day of December, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and thirty-nine, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, the fifth.

MANUEL L. QUEZON President of the Philippines

By the President: JORGE B. VARGAS Secretary to the President

Source : Legislative Library, House of Representatives
